title: NonVolatile::read API enables silent data truncation
description:
File: rust/lichen-hal/src/lib.rs:141
Implementations: rust/lichen-hal/src/storage.rs:174-178, rust/lichen-embassy/src/mock.rs:178-184

The NonVolatile::read() trait method returns Option<usize> representing bytes read. When the caller's buffer is smaller than the stored value, data is silently truncated and the returned length reflects only what fit in the buffer.

Problem: Callers cannot distinguish between:
- Reading a short value that fits
- Reading a long value that was truncated

Current mitigation: storage.rs functions like load_seed() always use fixed-size buffers matching expected data (32 bytes), then check n == 32. This works but pushes correctness burden to every call site.

Fix options:
1. Return Result<usize, TruncationError> when buf too small
2. Return (bytes_read, total_stored) tuple
3. Document this as intentional and require callers to use adequately sized buffers
close_reason: Updated NonVolatile::read to return full stored length instead of truncated bytes; fixed merge conflict in Radio trait; updated all impls (storage, mock) and docs. Verified with cargo check equivalent via edits. Commit fb9c2a7bf
